description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with five visible floors and a mansard roof featuring dormer windows. The facade is symmetrical, with evenly spaced rectangular windows and subtle decorative elements such as window sills and cornices. The ground floor is distinguished by a more pronounced stonework pattern and a central entrance with a small overhang. The building lacks balconies but includes bay window projections on either side of the main entrance. The roofline is punctuated by four dormer windows, adding character to the uppermost floor. The structure is set on a flat base, suggesting an urban placement with sidewalks or adjacent streets.
description: A five-story apartment building with a mansard roof, symmetrical windows, and a central entrance, featuring bay window projections but no balconies.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and bay window projections on both sides.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the building's depth, plain wall surfaces, and the sloped mansard roof with dormer windows.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar window arrangement as the front but lacking an entrance and bay windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side with plain walls and visible roof slope.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly symmetrical grid pattern, evenly spaced both vertically and horizontally across each facade. Each floor features a consistent number of windows, contributing to a balanced and orderly appearance.
